zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Stanley VI於1385年出生在英國爾胡頓時，他的父親Sir William de Stanley IV17歲，母親Margery de Hooton22歲。他於1410年與Alicia Houghton在英國爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他在英國爾被列為sheriff of cheshire。他去世於1466年2月17日在他的家鄉享年81歲，埋葬在英國爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Stanley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Stanley, including those in Yorkshire, Staffordshire, Derbyshire, Durham, Gloucestershire, and Wiltshire, named with Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. This English name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century where it was Gaelicized as de Stainléigh.

English: possibly also a variant of Stoneley, a habitational name from Stoneley Green in Burland (Cheshire), Stoneleigh (Warwickshire), or Stonely in Kimbolton (Huntingdonshire), all named with Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.
Americanized form of any of various like-sounding names in other languages, for example Polish Stanislawski , Greek Anastasiou , and Serbian Stojadinov (patronymic from the personal name Stojadin).

variants.name: Stanislawski
meanings: Polish (Stanisławski): habitational name for someone from any of various places called Stanisław, Stanisławów, or Stanisławice, named with the personal name Stanisław (see Stanislav ).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Stanley VI was born in 1385, in Hooton, Cheshire, England, his father, Sir William de Stanley IV, was 17 and his mother, Margery de Hooton, was 22. He married Alicia Houghton in 1410, in Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 8 daughters. His occupation is listed as sheriff of cheshire in Cheshire, England. He died on 17 February 1466, in his hometown, at the age of 81, and was buried in Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Stanley VI est né en 1385, à Hooton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Stanley IV, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Margery de Hooton, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Alicia Houghton en 1410, à Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 8 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of cheshire à Cheshire, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 17 février 1466, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 81 ans, et a été enterré à Cheshire, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: William de Stanley VI(이)가 1385년 영국 체실르 후턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William de Stanley IV(은)는 17세였고, 그의 어머니 Margery de Hooton(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1410년 영국 체실르에서 Alicia Houghton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 체실르에서 sheriff of cheshire이었다. 그는 1466년 2월 17일 그의 고향에서 81세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de Stanley VI nasceu em 1385, em Hooton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Stanley IV, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Margery de Hooton, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Alicia Houghton em 1410, em Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 8 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como sheriff of cheshire em Cheshire, Inglaterra. ru.Penrhyn, Llandygia, Caernarvanshire, Wal: пенрин би конвеи, каирнарфоншир, Уэльс, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Stanley VI родился в 1385 г. в местности хутон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William de Stanley IV было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery de Hooton, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Alicia Houghton в 1410 г. в местности чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 8 дочерей.  Он работал по профессии sheriff of cheshire в местности чешир, Англия. Он умер 17 февраля 1466 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 81 год и был похоронен в местности чешир, Англия.

Se casó con Alicia Houghton en 1410, en Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 8 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era sheriff of cheshire en Cheshire, Inglaterra. Murió el 17 de febrero de 1466, en su pueblo, a la edad de 81 años, y fue sepultado en Cheshire, Inglaterra.
